records.inventionTitle: Adaptive Thermal Management For Battery Packs
records.abstractText: A control system dynamically allocates coolant flow across battery modules using predictive cell temperature models and charge-state forecasts.
records.claimsText: 1. A battery thermal management system comprising a manifold, controllable valves, and a model-predictive controller configured to estimate future module temperature. 2. The system of claim 1 wherein flow is adjusted based on charging profile and estimated degradation rate.
records.summaryText: The disclosure describes balancing cooling efficiency and battery lifetime by actively routing coolant to modules with projected thermal stress.
records.descriptionText: Sensor arrays capture module temperature, current draw, and ambient conditions. A predictive model computes module heat rise over a forecast horizon and a valve optimization routine minimizes peak module temperature.
records.backgroundText: Conventional battery cooling routes uniform coolant flow and cannot respond to localized thermal spikes.

records.inventionTitle: Sparse Attention Accelerator For Transformer Inference
records.abstractText: Hardware and compiler techniques for skipping low-impact attention blocks during autoregressive token generation.
records.claimsText: 1. A method for transformer inference comprising ranking attention heads by contribution score and executing only a selected subset per token. 2. A scheduler that promotes or demotes heads based on observed perplexity drift.
records.summaryText: The approach improves inference throughput while maintaining quality by adaptive sparse head execution.
records.descriptionText: The accelerator includes a contribution estimator, head-selection unit, and mixed-precision matrix engine. A runtime controller applies quality thresholds and fallback full-head execution when drift exceeds guardrails.
records.backgroundText: Large language model inference cost grows with sequence length and dense attention execution.

records.inventionTitle: Computer Vision Guided Robotic Orchard Harvesting
records.abstractText: An orchard robot identifies fruit ripeness and manipulates a compliant end effector to reduce bruising during harvest.
records.claimsText: 1. A harvesting robot comprising a ripeness classifier and a deformable gripper controlled by force feedback. 2. A picking trajectory planner that adapts to occlusions and branch motion.
records.summaryText: Vision, force sensing, and adaptive path planning improve harvest precision and fruit quality.
records.descriptionText: Stereo cameras detect fruit candidates and estimate ripeness scores. A planner computes grasp approach vectors and updates trajectories using real-time branch movement estimation.
records.backgroundText: Manual orchard harvesting is labor intensive and existing rigid grippers often damage produce.
